title: Kasbeswari Kali Mata
tagline: Kasbeswari Kali Mata — spiritual town of Tripura

overview.short: Kasbeswari Kali Mata or Kamalasagar Kali Temple was built on a hill-top by Maharaja Dhanya Manikya in the late 15th century.
overview.description: Kasbeswari Kali Mata or Kamalasagar Kali Temple was built on a hill-top by Maharaja Dhanya Manikya in the late 15th century. It is situated just beside the Bangladesh border, The lake, Kamalasagar, in front of this temple rightly enhances its beauty. Kamalasagar Temple is located about 27 km from Agartala in a small village called Kasba . It falls on Indo- Bangladesh border.

topPlaces.name: Kasba Upazila
topPlaces.description: Kasba (Bengali: কসবা) is an upazila of Brahmanbaria District, Chittagong Division in east-central Bangladesh. It is located approximately 150 km north of Chittagong and was part of greater Cumilla District until 1984.

topPlaces.name: Brahmanbaria-4
topPlaces.description: Brahmanbaria-4 is a constituency represented in the Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament) of Bangladesh.

topPlaces.name: Kamalasagar
topPlaces.description: Kamalasagar is an artificial lake in Tripura, India, constructed by King Dhanya Manikya of Tripura in the 15th century and is a popular spot for picnickers. A mela is held there every October during the Navaratri festival.

topPlaces.name: Mondobhag train collision
topPlaces.description: The Mondobhag train collision occurred on 12 November 2019 at Mondobhag railway station, Kasba, Bangladesh. Two trains were involved.

topPlaces.name: Bagra, Chittagong
topPlaces.description: Bagra (also, Bāgra) is a village in Chittagong Division, Bangladesh.

topPlaces.name: Purquil
topPlaces.description: Purquil is a village in the east-central part of Bangladesh. It is located at Mehari Union of Kasba Upazila in Brahmanbaria District of Chittagong Division.

topPlaces.name: Kheora
topPlaces.description: Kheore is a village in the east-central part of Bangladesh. It is located at Mehari Union under Kasba Upazila in Brahmanbaria District of Chittagong division.

items.note: Kasba (Bengali: কসবা) is an upazila of Brahmanbaria District, Chittagong Division in east-central Bangladesh. It is located approximately 150 km north of Chittagong and was part of greater Cumilla District until 1984.
Brahmanbaria-4 is a constituency represented in the Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament) of Bangladesh.

Kamalasagar is an artificial lake in Tripura, India, constructed by King Dhanya Manikya of Tripura in the 15th century and is a popular spot for picnickers. A mela is held there every October during the Navaratri festival.
The Mondobhag train collision occurred on 12 November 2019 at Mondobhag railway station, Kasba, Bangladesh. Two trains were involved.
Bagra (also, Bāgra) is a village in Chittagong Division, Bangladesh.
Purquil is a village in the east-central part of Bangladesh. It is located at Mehari Union of Kasba Upazila in Brahmanbaria District of Chittagong Division.
Kheore is a village in the east-central part of Bangladesh. It is located at Mehari Union under Kasba Upazila in Brahmanbaria District of Chittagong division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kasbeswari Kali Mata?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kasbeswari Kali Mata is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kasbeswari Kali Mata?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharaja Bir Bikram Airport, Agartala (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Agartala Railway Station (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kasbeswari Kali Mata?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kasbeswari Kali Mata at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kasbeswari Kali Mata?
faq.a: Stays in Kasbeswari Kali Mata start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kasbeswari Kali Mata?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kasba Upazila, Brahmanbaria-4, Kamalasagar, Mondobhag train collision, Bagra, Chittagong.
